# Anti-Patterns — Language That Fails

**Anti-Pattern 1: Command and Control. Tell people what to do. They lose initiative.**

**Anti-Pattern 2: Mixing Red and Blue. Asking for creative input during execution mode.**

**Case: The Micromanager. A leader who controls every detail destroys engagement.**

**Case: The Empty Empowerment. Giving freedom without boundaries creates chaos.**

**Case: The Blame Culture. Blaming individuals instead of fixing the system.**

**Case: The Rush. Never completing the cycle. No reflection, no learning.**
---

**Case: The Redwork Swamp. Teams stuck in execution mode never have time to think.**
**Case: The Bluework Trap. Teams that spend all their time planning never execute.**
**Case: The Sandwich Feedback. Good-bad-good feedback is confusing. Be direct.**
**Case: The Yes Culture. When everyone says yes, no one is thinking critically.**
**Case: The False Urgency. Creating artificial urgency destroys bluework time.**
**Case: The Leader's Solution. When the leader proposes solutions, the team stops thinking.**
**Case: The Hero Leader. Leaders who save the day create dependency, not empowerment.**
**Case: The No-Zero Culture. Zero defects is a myth. Learning from failure is better than avoiding it.**
**Case: The Accountability. Empowerment requires accountability. I intend to means I am responsible.**
